title: austrobaileyanae
intro: The austrobaileyanae rank is a clade within the plant kingdom, specifically a group of flowering plants that shares a common ancestor and distinct characteristics. It is a subdivision of the flowering plant orders, often defined by molecular and morphological traits. Examples of orders that have been classified as austrobaileyanae include the Asparagales, Malpighiales, and Poales, which are diverse groups of plants with distinct growth habits and reproductive structures. This clade provides a way to group and understand the evolutionary relationships within the flowering plant lineage.

orders.order_desc: Arales is a former order of flowering plants that is now considered obsolete, but it was previously classified within the subclass Rosidae of the class Magnoliopsida in the division Magnoliophyta of the kingdom Plantae.
